steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The passage describes Pierce's 1879 seed-burial experiment, notes that researchers are currently conducting periodic checks, and reports a 2021 retrieval. The key signal is the present-tense framing: this is not a completed historical account but an active, continuing investigation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To discuss an ongoing experiment: the passage presents a 19th-century setup and then immediately shifts to present-tense activity (“researchers are conducting” periodic checks) and a 2021 bottle retrieval, establishing the experiment as still underway. ✓
  • Choice B — To explain how to get rid of certain plants: removing or eliminating plants is never mentioned. The passage only tracks whether seeds remain viable. ✗
  • Choice C — To compare two experiments addressing the same questions: only one experiment — Pierce's — is described. No second experiment is introduced for comparison. ✗
  • Choice D — To describe difficulties associated with preserving seeds: the passage reports outcomes (most seeds stopped germinating after 60 years; Verbascum seeds remained viable) without framing those outcomes as difficulties or challenges to preservation. ✗
That matches Choice A — To discuss an ongoing experiment.
